attributes.prompt: risk to public credibility if cardinal-harness is shared widely as a serious empirical artifact
how much this issue blocks concrete falsifiable live evidence rather than merely improving polish
leverage of fixing this issue for making the project safer to share widely

entities.text: Risk: the repo has strong synthetic/offline receipts but still needs a larger preserved live-LLM benchmark pack across task families, models, costs, and traces before making broad empirical claims.
Risk: cargo audit currently exits with allowed transitive warnings for paste, anyhow, and rand. These are not necessarily exploitable here, but public release readers will notice them.
Risk: OpenRouter model IDs, pricing, supported parameters, and logprob behavior change over time. Checked-in policy recipes can become stale unless refreshed and dated.
Risk: provider_cost_nanodollars is exact only when OpenRouter reports cost or local pricing table has an exact entry. Fallback estimates must stay labeled or readers may overtrust cost comparisons.
Risk: cached comparisons improve reproducibility and cost, but a public receipt must make cache hits, model IDs, prompt hashes, and request hashes obvious enough to audit.
Risk: Likert and ordinal controls are present locally, but the live evidence should compare multiple real prompt families and budget policies rather than one weak baseline.
Risk: the crate exposes advanced reranking, trace, policy, cache, gateway, and evaluation surfaces. A first-time public user may struggle unless the core path stays visibly small.
